blockingIssues.description: `.factory/services.yaml` still defines the mission `test` hard gate as `npm --prefix apps/desktop run test && xcodebuild test ...`, so the foundation baseline remains coupled to unrelated red desktop suites instead of the promised green iOS-only simulator path.

Lane detail now disables diff entry points while offline or syncing, even though the validation contract says cached lane context should remain inspectable and only live-only git mutations should gate in those states.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented the shared `ADEMobilePrimitives.swift` layer in `.factory/library/ios-design-system.md`, including `ADEGlassSection`, `ADEGlassChip`, `ADEGlassActionButton`, and `ADEMatchedTransitionScope` so later iOS workers reuse the shared mobile primitive layer.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reconcile the mission hard-gate guidance with `.factory/services.yaml` by explicitly pointing iOS-only workers at the `ios-*` commands or otherwise clarifying that the generic `test` command is not the foundation gate when unrelated desktop suites are known red.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `foundation-fix-ios-baseline-tests` and `foundation-lanes-benchmark-polish` flagged the mismatch between `/Users/admin/.factory/missions/3d6b3fd2-ee5a-4122-83b2-d6dff0c07f04/AGENTS.md` saying unrelated desktop failures should not block iOS-only work and `/Users/admin/Projects/ADE/.ade/worktrees/mobile-droid-attempt-bbdcd095/.factory/services.yaml:4` still running `npm --prefix apps/desktop run test && xcodebuild test ...`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the `sync-parity-worker` baseline-failure procedure so features whose explicit job is to repair a known-red validation path are not told to abort back to the orchestrator before diagnosing and fixing that baseline.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `foundation-fix-ios-baseline-tests` handoff recorded a deliberate deviation because the current skill text says to stop when the baseline command fails, but this feature existed specifically to repair the failing iOS baseline.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten `ios-worker` manual-verification guidance for shared UI/polish features so workers either perform a real simulator spot-check when available or explicitly record why only code-path inspection was possible.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `foundation-shared-mobile-design-system-and-performance-primitives` review found the skill encourages the strongest available manual verification, but the worker's recorded checks stayed at code-inspection level for a feature that claimed visible mobile polish primitives.

rejectedObservations.observation: Mission guidance and the checked-in service manifest disagree about whether the foundation hard gate is iOS-only.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate; both failing reviews reported the same services-vs-guidance mismatch and it is represented once in blocking issues and suggested guidance updates

rejectedObservations.observation: `init.sh` generated additional `.factory/library` and skill files during the baseline-repair feature, which caused unrelated factory artifacts to appear in that feature's commit.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous; the review did not establish a durable codebase rule change beyond this one-time bootstrap sequence
